description: Configuration file for the resist-lint custom linter.

$schema.description: Path to the JSON Schema for IDE autocomplete.

exclude.description: Glob patterns to exclude from linting (e.g. "*.test.ts", "*.d.ts").

include.description: Paths to include in linting (relative to workspace root).

overrides.description: File-specific rule overrides. Last matching override wins.

files.description: Glob patterns matching files to apply these overrides to.

rules.description: Rule-level severity overrides for matched files.

ruleOptions.description:
Per-rule configuration options. Keys are rule IDs, values are option objects.

Common options:
- categories: string[] — override rule categories for filtering
- stages: string[] — override pipeline stages (lint, check, pre-commit, build, ci, test)

rules.description:
Rule ID → severity mapping. Unlisted rules default to "error".

Available rules:
- `comments/no-lint-disable`: Forbids lint-suppression comments (eslint-disable, oxlint-ignore, @ts-ignore, etc.)
- `comments/require-blank-line-groups`: Require blank lines between declaration and control flow statement groups
- `comments/require-section-marker-style`: Section markers must use the canonical // === style
- `comments/require-section-order`: File sections marked with // === headers must follow canonical order
- `imports/no-barrel-files`: Forbids barrel files (index.ts with re-exports)
- `imports/no-js-extension`: Import paths must not use .js extension — use .ts or omit
- `imports/no-raw-json`: Use safeStringify/parseJsonWithComments instead of JSON.stringify/parse
- `imports/no-raw-node-imports`: Use @/utils/core shared utilities instead of raw node:* imports
- `imports/no-reexport`: Forbids re-exports — always import from canonical source
- `imports/no-relative-imports`: Import paths must not use relative paths (./ or ../)
- `imports/require-import-groups`: Require blank lines between import groups (node, external, workspace)
- `jsdoc/param-type-match`: @param {Type} must match the actual TypeScript type annotation
- `jsdoc/require-example`: Exported functions must have an @example block with ```typescript``` fence
- `jsdoc/require-jsdoc`: Exported functions and types must have a JSDoc comment
- `jsdoc/require-module`: Every TypeScript file must have a top-level /** @module */ JSDoc comment
- `jsdoc/require-param`: Every function parameter must have a matching @param in JSDoc
- `jsdoc/require-returns`: Exported functions with non-void return types must have @returns {Type}
- `jsdoc/require-schema-link`: Types derived from Valibot schemas must include {@link SchemaName} in JSDoc
- `jsdoc/validate-example`: TypeScript code in @example blocks must be syntactically valid
- `naming/camel-case-vars`: Variables and functions must use camelCase
- `naming/constant-screaming-case`: Top-level const with literal init must use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
- `naming/pascal-case-types`: Types, interfaces, and enums must use PascalCase
- `naming/svelte-file-pascal-case`: Svelte files must have PascalCase filenames
- `naming/ts-file-kebab-case`: TypeScript files must have kebab-case filenames
- `result/check-before-access`: Result .ok must be checked before accessing .data or .error
- `result/no-ignore-result`: Result return values must be captured — do not ignore them
- `result/no-redundant-ok-guard`: Redundant .ok guard — both branches return the same variable
- `result/no-result-fallback`: Do not silently discard Result errors with fallback values
- `result/no-ternary-fallback`: Forbids result.ok ? result.data : fallback — use if (!result.ok) return result
- `result/require-ok-return`: Functions returning Result<T> must use ok()/okUnchecked()/err() in returns
- `result/require-result-type`: Exported functions that might fail should return Result<T>
- `result/validate-function-input`: Exported/handler functions should validate input with safeParse()
- `testing/multi-export-a`: Multi-export fixture rule A (no-op).
- `testing/multi-export-b`: Multi-export fixture rule B (no-op).
- `testing/named-export-a`: Named export fixture rule (no-op).
- `testing/require-colocated-tests`: Files exporting functions must have a colocated .test.ts file
- `typescript/no-bare-as-cast`: Every `as` cast must have an inline comment explaining why
- `typescript/no-bare-data-types`: Data types must use Valibot schemas, not interface/type literals
- `typescript/no-builtin-types`: Use Valibot types (Str, Num, Bool, Void) instead of TypeScript builtins
- `typescript/no-default-params`: Function parameters must not have defaults or optional markers — use Valibot schemas
- `typescript/no-empty-catch`: Catch blocks must use fromUnknownError() + err() pattern
- `typescript/no-generic-function-type`: Function types must have specific parameter and return types, not generic unknown
- `typescript/no-module-side-effects`: Top-level code must not throw or execute side-effect calls on import
- `typescript/no-throw`: Forbids throw statements — use return err() instead
- `typescript/no-union-null`: Use Valibot nullable/optional types instead of | null / | undefined
- `typescript/no-union-params`: Function parameters must not use | union types — use Valibot schemas instead
- `typescript/require-const-comment`: Every top-level const must have a preceding comment
- `typescript/require-function-schema`: Use functionSchema() from @/schemas/function instead of v.custom for function types
- `typescript/require-non-negative-integer`: .length must be typed as NonNegativeInteger, not Num
- `typescript/require-return-type`: Every function must have an explicit return type annotation
- `typescript/require-type-annotation`: Every const/let declaration and function parameter must have a type annotation
- `valibot/namespace-import`: Valibot must be imported as namespace: import * as v from 'valibot'
- `valibot/no-direct-safeparse`: Forbids v.safeParse() — use safeParse from @/utils/result/safe instead
- `valibot/no-duplicate-schema`: Schema field patterns appearing in 3+ files should be in @/schemas/common
- `valibot/no-generic-string-schema`: v.pipe(v.string(), v.minLength(1)) is too generic — add semantic constraints
- `valibot/no-parse`: Forbids v.parse() — use safeParse from @/utils/result/safe instead
- `valibot/prefer-shared-schema`: Use shared schemas (PathSchema, UrlStringSchema, etc.) for matching field names
- `valibot/prefer-template-literal`: Prefer templateLiteral() over v.pipe(v.string(), v.regex()) for structured string patterns
- `valibot/require-field-docs`: Every property in v.strictObject() must have a JSDoc comment
- `valibot/require-generic-schema`: Schemas used by generic types should use generic() from @/schemas/generic
- `valibot/require-min-length`: Bare v.string() in strictObject fields should have v.minLength(1)
- `valibot/require-schema-suffix`: Valibot schema const names must end in "Schema"
- `valibot/require-strict-object`: Forbids v.object() — use v.strictObject() instead
- `package/no-hoisted-dep`: Hoisted dependencies must not appear in sub-package dependencies
- `package/no-peer-deps`: Private workspace packages must not have peerDependencies
- `package/no-root-only-scripts`: qa:format, qa:format:check, qa:lint belong only in workspace root
- `package/no-tsc-dependency`: Packages using tsgo should not depend on typescript
- `package/no-vitest-config`: Sub-packages must not have vitest.config.ts — use root config with --project
- `package/no-workspace-dep`: Workspace-root-level deps must not appear in sub-package devDependencies
- `package/no-workspace-self-ref`: Sub-packages must not have workspace:* dependencies — use tsconfig paths
- `package/require-project-test`: Test scripts must use pnpm -w exec vitest run --project <name>
- `package/require-readme`: Every sub-package must have a validated README.md
- `package/require-shared-config`: Config files must use shared config tooling
- `package/require-standard-scripts`: Sub-packages must have all standard scripts
- `package/require-tsgo`: qa:type-check must use tsgo, not tsc
- `package/valid-project-ref`: Scripts with --project must reference a valid vitest project name
- `package/valid-tsconfig`: Sub-package tsconfig.json must extend root and include src

title: resist-lint configuration
